steps.title: Enter two years of values
steps.content: At $x=0$, the house is worth $174{,}000$. A $2.4\%$ annual increase means the one-year value is $174{,}000(1.024)=178{,}176$.

steps.title: Fit the exponential model
steps.content: Run $y_1 \sim a b^{x_1}$ in Desmos. The regression gives $a=174000$ and $b=1.024$, so the model is $y=174000(1.024)^x$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Choice A uses $0.024$ as the whole multiplier, and choice B uses $0.24$; both would shrink the value. Choice D uses $1.24$, a $24\%$ increase. Choice C uses $1.024$, the correct yearly multiplier. Choice C is correct.
